name: Walker, Nancy
resolved_employer: State of Texas — Policy Advisor (retired / currently not employed); also principal of Nancy Walker Consulting LLC, a small Austin business-consulting firm registered at her 78749 home address
evidence: FEC-derived individual-contribution records show a NANCY WALKER at zip 78749 giving as 'STATE OF TEXAS/POLICY ADVISOR' ($25 to Hillary for America, 10/14/2016) — a direct name+zip match to this donor, who now self-reports 'Not Employed', consistent with retirement from state service. Travis County property records list NANCY J WALKER at the registered address of Nancy Walker Consulting LLC (management/technical consulting), but that link rests on an address match rather than confirmed identity, hence medium confidence. Her 407 recorded federal donations at a 1.0 Democratic lean fit a high-frequency small-dollar ActBlue-style giving pattern.

name: Greer, Denise
resolved_employer: Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA), El Paso, TX
evidence: A medical directory listing for 'Denise Greer, CRNA, El Paso, TX' matches the donor's reported occupation and city. The reported employer 'Heart Hospital' could not be tied to any El Paso facility — no hospital by that name operates there — so she may be a travel or contract CRNA, or the string may reference an out-of-market facility such as The Heart Hospital Baylor in DFW. Occupation + city corroborate identity; the specific zip does not, so confidence is medium. FEC search returned no records for this individual and no ideological or industry PAC giving on any side.

name: Gayle, Jackson
resolved_employer: Travis County District Attorney's Office — Assistant District Attorney (Texas Bar record also lists Texas Legal Services Center as firm of record)
evidence: Name is reversed in the filing: this is Jackson Parker Gayle, Texas Bar #24107780, licensed since 5/4/2018, South Texas College of Law Houston (2017), Austin-based — a direct match to the reported 'Lawyer, ADA' occupation and TCDAO employer. His State Bar record lists Texas Legal Services Center (Austin legal-aid nonprofit) as firm of record. NOT RECORDED: several search-engine summaries asserted he 'filed to run for Austin City Council, District 1'; no primary source corroborates this (the Austin filing period does not open until 7/20/2026 and no candidate roster lists him), so it is excluded as likely summarizer conflation.

name: Beall, Jonathan
resolved_employer: Sertodo Copper — founder and director; Austin-based handcrafted copper goods maker with workshops in Austin, TX and Santa Clara del Cobre, Michoacán, Mexico
evidence: Jonathan Beall founded Sertodo Copper in 1997 after a dot-com layoff in Austin and an apprenticeship with Michoacán coppersmiths; he runs it as an international artisan cooperative with 30+ employees producing cookware and barware from recycled copper. Employer string 'Sertodo' and occupation 'Copper' match exactly, and prior political giving records place him in Austin (e.g. a $250 contribution to Bernie 2016 listed as Sertodo Copper, Austin TX). Full reads of his Voyage Austin interview and the Sertodo about page disclose no board seats or advocacy affiliations.

name: Angle, Matt
resolved_employer: Angle Strategies, Inc. (Arlington, VA) — principal; founder and Director of the Lone Star Project, a Texas Democratic political research and strategic communications organization
evidence: Confirmed as the Texas Democratic operative: Matt Angle, former chief of staff to U.S. Rep. Martin Frost, founded the Lone Star Project in 2005 and serves as its Director, and is principal of Angle Strategies, Inc. — matching the filing's Arlington VA 22207 address, 'Consultant' occupation, and 'Angle Strategies' employer string. FEC records show federal giving from Matthew Angle, self-employed consultant, Arlington VA 22207 (e.g. $500 to the Biden Victory Fund, 08/24/2020); no ideological-PAC contributions surfaced on any side. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found — his work is PAC/research and campaign consulting, not registered lobbying.

affiliations.snippet: "LSP Director, Matt Angle and LSP legal counsel are recognized among the most informed and credible Texas voices on redistricting and voting rights matters." Founded 2005 as a Texas Democratic political research and strategic communications organization.

Angle is the principal of Angle Strategies, Inc., an Arlington, VA political consulting firm, and has served as director of the Texas Democratic Trust and as a political/redistricting consultant to America Votes.

name: Lierman, Brooke
resolved_employer: State of Maryland — 34th Comptroller of Maryland (elected 2022, took office January 2023); previously counsel at Brown, Goldstein & Levy LLP and member of the Maryland House of Delegates, District 46 (2015–2023)
evidence: Confirmed as the sitting Comptroller of Maryland: occupation 'Comptroller', employer 'Maryland', and Baltimore MD 21231 residence all match — she is the first woman independently elected to a Maryland constitutional office. An attorney (Dartmouth BA, University of Texas School of Law JD) who practiced civil rights and disability rights law before eight years in the Maryland House. As a public official her political giving is a matter of public record; no ideological-PAC ties surfaced. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

affiliations.org: Maryland General Assembly / State of Maryland
affiliations.snippet: Lierman served in the Maryland House of Delegates from 2015 to 2023 on the Appropriations and Environment and Transportation committees, and has been the 34th Comptroller of Maryland since 2023.

Lierman served on the board of Advocates for Children and Youth, a Maryland child-policy advocacy organization.

name: Davis, Wendy

Box; the entry could not be independently opened and read, so it is excluded as unverified.

name: Whatley, Melba
resolved_employer: MDW Interests, Ltd. — principal; private oil, gas and real estate investment firm (filed as 'Unemployed')
